assumptions: No Harness account, organization, project, entitlement, source repository, Git-provider connection, connector, pipeline, stage, build infrastructure, command step, or execution is assumed.
A browser, working identity, network connection, and access to a source-code repository are available.
No signup, email verification, SSO, repository authorization, connector creation, pipeline creation, or build execution was performed during research.

developer_goal: Start without a Harness account, create or enter a Harness account, provide a source-code repository, create an inline CI pipeline, add a Build stage on Cloud infrastructure, run the documented echo command, execute the pipeline from a Git branch, and observe the first build complete.

first_success.outcome: A Cloud CI build for a selected Git branch executes the configured Run step containing `echo "Harness CI is fast"`.
first_success.observable_signal: The pipeline execution reaches a successful terminal state and its build logs contain the Run step output `Harness CI is fast`.

route_selection.surface: Harness CI first Cloud build pipeline
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Harness CI first Cloud build pipeline
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Harness CD first deployment
candidates.reason: A deployment adds service, environment, infrastructure, manifest, and target credentials beyond the CI guide's earlier executable outcome.

candidates.name: Remote pipeline storage
candidates.reason: A Remote pipeline adds Git connector and repository-location setup. Inline is offered during creation and requires fewer dependencies.

candidates.name: Self-managed build infrastructure
candidates.reason: The onboarding guide uses Cloud build infrastructure with default operating-system and architecture choices, while self-managed infrastructure adds installation and operation.

required_path.action: Complete Harness account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Harness account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Harness candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The pipeline execution reaches a successful terminal state and its build logs contain the Run step output `Harness CI is fast`.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Create a Harness account or sign in to an existing account before accessing the Continuous Integration module.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S3']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'access', 'requirement': 'Have access to a Harness organization and project in which a CI pipeline can be created.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S3']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'configuration', 'requirement': 'Have a source-code repository and branch available through Harness Code Repository or a supported third-party Git provider.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

external_gates.description: The CI module requires a Harness account and authenticated console session.
The public official sources do not expose the complete no-account signup and verification sequence.
The developer must have or create the correct organization and project context before pipeline creation.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Run your first Harness CI pipeline.

excluded.item: Repository creation and application code, compilation and test steps, cache and service dependencies, secrets, artifact publication, pull-request and webhook triggers, failure handling, quality gates,
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Get Started with Harness CI
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

sources.title: Harness Continuous Integration overview
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Harness Continuous Integration overview

sources.title: CI pipeline creation overview
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for CI pipeline creation overview

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Harness show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
